L1.area: Remote service invocation and RPC-style communication layer

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where untrusted data received over the network is deserialized using Java's native serialization mechanism without adequate validation or filtering of the deserialized object types. An attacker can send a crafted serialized payload to trigger arbitrary code execution on the server.

L3.area: HTTP-based remote method invocation service exporters and their serialization handling
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists in the HTTP-based remote method invocation infrastructure where incoming serialized Java objects from HTTP requests are deserialized without restriction on allowable types. An attacker can craft a malicious serialized object payload and send it to an exposed HTTP remoting endpoint to achieve remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The HTTP invoker service exporters deserialize incoming Java objects from HTTP requests using ObjectInputStream.readObject() without any type filtering or validation, allowing an attacker to send crafted serialized payloads that trigger arbitrary code execution.
